entities.label: has Subcontractor

entities.label: is Production Of

workspace.domainContext: The eProcurement Ontology (ePO) 5.2.0 models concepts and relationships across the European public-procurement lifecycle, including access, submission, ordering, catalogue, fulfilment, invoicing and payment. Domain classes and properties use the epo namespace (http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#). References to controlled vocabularies and reusable European data models may use SKOS, ADMS, ORG and related namespaces.
workspace.generationGuidance: Treat each normative statement as the complete source of constraints. Preserve explicit cardinalities such as exactly one, at least one and at most one. Use sh:class and sh:nodeKind sh:IRI for object-property ranges, and use the ontology datatype for literal properties. When text permits either a plain or language-tagged string, represent both alternatives with sh:or. Reuse only ontology IRIs present in the supplied context, target the named ePO class, and do not turn explanatory background sentences into additional constraints.

batch.content:
# ePO Data Constraints

- Ontology: eProcurement Ontology (ePO) 5.2.0
- Author: DE-TUM NL2SHACL-Dataset; adapted as a SHARD example
- Date: 2026-07-21
- Description: Ten representative public-procurement data constraints aligned with ePO.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-1
- Title: ESPD Request - concerns Procedure, refers To Notice, specifies Procurement Criterion

### Data constraint

When curating an ESPD Request, which is a document issued by a buyer defining how the exclusion grounds and selection criteria should be addressed in a European Single Procurement Document for a specific procurement, several strict relationships must be established. The request must concern exactly one Procedure, representing the legally defined set of administrative activities conducted to conclude one or more contracts, and this relationship must be provided as a specific identifier URL. Furthermore, the request must specify at least one Procurement Criterion to be used, which must also be linked via a specific identifier URL. Finally, the ESPD Request may refer to a Notice, which is a document published by the buyer about market opportunities and results, and any such reference must similarly be recorded using a specific identifier URL.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-5
- Title: Selected Candidate List - contains Candidate, has Start Date

### Data constraint

When defining a Selected Candidate List, which serves as a formal record of candidates admitted to take part in the award phases of procurements, specific data rules apply. The list may include candidates, each represented by a direct link to a Candidate entity, defined as an agent that has sought an invitation or has been invited to participate in specialized procedures such as restricted procedures, competitive dialogues, or innovation partnerships. Additionally, the list may specify at most one start date to indicate the beginning date of the record. If provided, this start date must be a specific identifier URL pointing to a time interval, representing a temporal entity with a defined extent or duration.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-8
- Title: Procurement Process Information - concerns Lot, concerns Procedure, is Competition Terminated, is DPS Terminated, is To Be Relaunched

### Data constraint

When defining Procurement Process Information, which captures the temporal unfolding or succession of procurement objects, several specific constraints apply. The record may include a direct link to at most one Lot, representing a qualitative, quantitative, or strategic subdivision of the goods, services, or works to be procured. Similarly, it may provide a specific identifier URL for at most one Procedure, which is a legally defined set of administrative activities conducted to conclude one or more contracts. Furthermore, the process information can specify up to one boolean indicator to state if the competition is terminated, meaning no further contracts will be awarded in this procedure; this can be instantiated in the post-award phase even if no contracts are awarded, provided a prior information notice for competition is signaled. It may also include a single boolean value indicating whether the Dynamic Purchase System is terminated, which can similarly be used in a contract award notice regardless of whether contracts were awarded. Finally, the record may contain at most one boolean indicator specifying whether the procurement object is to be relaunched, a detail that can also be recorded during the post-award phase.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-12
- Title: Contractor - foresees Subcontractor, has Subcontractor, needs To Be A Winner

### Data constraint

When defining a Contractor, which represents the role of an agent that has signed a contract with a buyer, specific data constraints apply to its relationships. A contractor record may include a direct link to at most one foreseen subcontractor, indicating that the organization plans to utilize a subcontractor. This distinction is particularly important in result notices to clearly associate the proposed subcontractor with the specific organization rather than the tenderer as a whole. Additionally, the contractor may provide a specific identifier URL to at most one actual subcontractor, which is an agent that has an agreement to perform part or all of the contract obligations. Finally, to enforce the business rule that a contractor must be a winner, the record may include a direct link to at most one winner entity, representing the role of the agent to whom a lot has been awarded.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-19
- Title: Order Response Information - has Status, is Specific To Order Response Line

### Data constraint

When defining Order Response Information, which captures details about an order response, the record must include exactly one status. This status must be provided as a specific identifier URL that links to a standardized concept representing the current state of the response. Additionally, the record may include information that is particular to an individual line item. When this is the case, it must provide a direct link to an Order Response Line, an entity that details the seller's ability to fulfill a given order line, such as indicating the acceptance or rejection of the item, or making a counter-offer.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-22
- Title: Line - has Additional Information, has Associated Document, specifies Item

### Data constraint

When defining a Line, which serves as a component of a document providing specific information, several strict data curation rules apply. Each line must specify exactly one item by providing a specific identifier URL that links directly to an abstract item, representing a procurement need such as an item, item request, or deliverable. Additionally, a line may optionally include a specific identifier URL linking to a single associated document, which represents a set of interrelated business information and metadata conveyed in any medium or form. Finally, the line may contain an optional single entry for additional information to provide supplementary data about the record, which must be formatted as standard text or a language-specific text string.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-25
- Title: Review Information - concerns Review Decision, concerns Review Request, has Element Reference, relates To E Form Section Identifier

### Data constraint

When defining Review Information, which captures details about a review decision or a review request, several specific relationships and references can be established. You may provide at most one direct link to a Review Decision, representing the formal information about the decision, and up to one direct link to a Review Request, which captures the details of the request to review the procedure. To connect this information to the broader document, you can include an element reference formatted as a URI that points directly to the relevant class instance within the current notice. Additionally, you may specify at most one direct link to an identifier that relates the review information to a specific eForms section, ensuring the data is accurately mapped to the corresponding part of the electronic form.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-32
- Title: Receipt Advice Line - has Oversupply Quantity, has Received Quantity, has Rejected Quantity, has Short Quantity, is Submitted For Despatch Line

### Data constraint

When defining a Receipt Advice Line, which provides details concerning the fulfilment of a Despatch Line, you must provide exactly one direct link to the specific Despatch Line for which it is submitted, representing the fulfilment details of an Order Line. Additionally, the record may optionally include up to one specific identifier URL for various quantity metrics, each linking to a Quantity class that represents a counted number of non-monetary units possibly including fractions. These optional metrics consist of a received quantity to indicate the delivered amount, a rejected quantity to indicate the non-accepted amount, an oversupply quantity to indicate any surplus, and a short quantity to indicate any deficit.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-40
- Title: Batch - has Batch ID, has Best Before Date, has Expiry Date, has Manufacture Date, is Production Of

### Data constraint

When defining a Batch, which represents a production lot in which item instances were produced, the record must include exactly one direct link to an Abstract Item—a gathering class for items, item requests, and deliverables related to a procurement need—to indicate the specific item that is manufactured. You may provide at most one batch ID, which must be a specific identifier URL pointing to an Identifier record assigned to this specific batch of the produced item. Furthermore, the batch may specify up to one manufacture date, formatted as a date and time, indicating the day of production. It can also include at most one best before date, formatted as a standard date, to specify the day until which the quality of the item will remain optimal, and at most one expiry date, also formatted as a standard date, to indicate the last day until which the item can be used.

---

## Rule

- Number: EPO-50
- Title: Qualification Criterion - has Qualification Criteria Stated In ESPD Request, has Qualification Criteria Stated In Notice, has Qualification Criteria Stated In Procurement Documents, includes National Criterion

### Data constraint

When defining a Qualification Criterion, which represents a standard used during the first stage of a procurement procedure, data curators can specify exactly where the criterion's details are formally documented. This is captured using up to three optional true/false flags: a single flag indicating whether the criterion is specified in the ESPD Request, a single flag denoting if it is specified in the Notice, and a single flag stating whether it is specified in the Procurement Documents. Additionally, the record may include a national criterion to indicate that it contains a sub-criterion specific to a given Member State. This is primarily used for purely national criteria to establish mappings from eCertis and corresponds to a UBL-2.3 sub-tendering criterion. If a national criterion is included, it must be provided as a direct link to another Qualification Criterion.

---
